placeholders.Subtitle 2: Phishing detection with SageMaker Serverless SFT

Text Placeholder 6: Concepts — SFT, LoRA, and what "serverless training" means here
Fine-tune — LoRA on Qwen3-4B with the SageMaker SDK (nb 01)
Evaluate — Custom Scorer baseline vs fine-tuned (nb 03 / 04)
Results — the lift, and the reasoning-model scoring trap
Take-home — vision variant + Bedrock serverless deploy

placeholders.Title 1: Why fine-tune a small model for this?
Content Placeholder 2: Phishing/scam detection is high-volume and latency-sensitive — you classify millions of pages, not chat with one
A generic frontier LLM is overkill, expensive per call, and not specialized to your label space
A 4B model LoRA-tuned on ~1.8k examples becomes a fast, cheap, specialized classifier you own
Single-token output (phish / benign) → trivial to evaluate exactly: accuracy = pred == truth
Easy to extend later: brand-target prediction, reasoning chains, JSON output

Content Placeholder 3: Show the model labeled prompt → completion pairs and adjust weights to match
4: Train small adapter matrices, freeze the base weights
Faster, cheaper, ~MBs not GBs — and CMI can merge it back
11: No instance type, count, or fleet to pick
SageMaker provisions the GPUs; you pay wallclock
SFTTrainer never asks for compute settings

placeholders.Text Placeholder 2: What makes it a good fit
Content Placeholder 3: Small enough to LoRA-tune end-to-end inside a ~75-min session
Capable enough that 1 epoch on 1.8k examples visibly lifts the classifier
In JumpStart as huggingface-reasoning-qwen3-4b — supports SFT/DPO/RLAIF/RLVR
Has a sibling VLM (qwen3-5-4b) → natural 'now try vision' stretch
Content Placeholder 5: It's a reasoning model — emits <think>…</think>, which bites the scorer (slide 15)
Bedrock CMI supports only Qwen3ForCausalLM — our SFT output qualifies
No Converse API for Qwen3 → use InvokeModel
Qwen license applies — you accept the EULA before training

placeholders.Content Placeholder 2:
# 666k labeled phishing/benign HTML+URL pairs (36.6 GB), streamed — never downloaded whole.
# Prep sliced 1k phish + 1k benign into a public HF dataset: gt2026workshop/phreshphish-2k

from datasets import load_dataset
ds = load_dataset("gt2026workshop/phreshphish-2k", name="text")

ds["train"][0]
# {
#   "prompt":     "You are a phishing detector. URL: ... Title: ... Forms: ... Visible text: ...",
#   "completion": "phish",          # <- single token, label-only on purpose
#   "label": "phish", "target_brand": "paypal", "sha256": "..."
# }

# SFTTrainer wants {"prompt":..., "completion":...} per line. Label-only → exact eval, fast.

placeholders.Title 1: Stage the data into the AI Registry
placeholders.Content Placeholder 2:
# 1. Upload the JSONL splits to S3 (default bucket: sagemaker-<region>-<acct>)
s3.upload_file("train.jsonl",      BUCKET, f"{PREFIX}/data/train.jsonl")
s3.upload_file("validation.jsonl", BUCKET, f"{PREFIX}/data/validation.jsonl")

# 2. Register both splits with the SageMaker AI Registry
from sagemaker.ai_registry.dataset import DataSet
train_dataset = DataSet.create(name="phreshphish-workshop-text-train", source=TRAIN_S3, wait=True)
val_dataset   = DataSet.create(name="phreshphish-workshop-text-val",   source=VAL_S3,   wait=True)

# 3. The registry collection the fine-tuned adapter will land in
from sagemaker.core.resources import ModelPackageGroup
mpg = ModelPackageGroup.create(model_package_group_name="qwen3-4b-phish-sft", ...)

from sagemaker.train.sft_trainer import SFTTrainer
from sagemaker.train.common import TrainingType

trainer = SFTTrainer(
    model="huggingface-reasoning-qwen3-4b",
    training_type=TrainingType.LORA,         # adapter, not full FT
    model_package_group=mpg,
    training_dataset=train_dataset.arn,
    validation_dataset=val_dataset.arn,
    s3_output_path=S3_OUTPUT_PATH,
    accept_eula=True,                        # Qwen3 license
    mlflow_resource_arn=MLFLOW_RESOURCE_ARN, # None => no curves
)   # no instance_type / count / fleet — compute is managed

training_job = trainer.train(wait=True)      # ~20-30 min, to MLflow

placeholders.Title 1: Watch it train — and the MLflow gotcha
Content Placeholder 2: Live loss curves: Studio → Applications → MLflow → experiment qwen3-4b-phish-sft, run workshop-run-01
You'll see train_loss and eval_loss per step/epoch (also mirrored to TensorBoard in S3)
Gotcha: if MLFLOW_RESOURCE_ARN is None the SDK tries to auto-CREATE an app — but the account is capped at 3
At quota that create fails silently → no curves, and eval jobs can fail with a malformed-ARN error
Fix: auto-discover an existing app with list_mlflow_apps and pass its ARN (all notebooks do this)

placeholders.Title 1: Custom Scorer: a tiny reward function
Content Placeholder 2: Evaluator = a Lambda returning 1.0 when the model's label matches ground truth, else 0.0
nb 03 registers the eval dataset + reward fn once; scores the BASE model (no fine-tune needed)
So run nb 03 DURING the 20–30 min training wait — it's not dead time
nb 04 reuses the SAME dataset + scorer, evaluate_base_model=True → base + fine-tuned in one job
Lambda needs a role trusted by lambda.amazonaws.com (exec role works if Lambda-assumable)
Cost: ~$5–15 SFT-LoRA + ~$1–3 if you spin a real-time endpoint → ~$6–20 per attendee
Each attendee: a SageMaker AI domain + Studio, exec role with AmazonSageMakerModelCustomizationCoreAccess
Accept the Qwen3 EULA in JumpStart before the session
One MLflow app to reuse (cap is 3) — notebooks auto-discover it
A Lambda-assumable role for the reward fn (eval notebooks 03/04)
Bedrock Custom Model Import is BLOCKED in the shared account by an SCP → take-home only
Same task, different modality: LoRA-tune the VLM huggingface-vlm-qwen3-5-4b on rendered page screenshots
Rationale: a typosquatted PayPal page is obvious visually even when the HTML is obfuscated
Dataset is multimodal — messages with image + text content; images staged to S3 by S3 URI
Caveat: PhreshPhish stores HTML only. Re-rendering in 2026 is degraded — JS-disabled SPAs, offline assets
~70–80% of renders succeed; of those ~half look page-like. Enough to DEMONSTRATE SFT on a VLM, not for production

Content Placeholder 3: Billed per-hour while the endpoint runs
Good for a fast in-session smoke test
Content Placeholder 5: Per-token + per-copy on-demand; scales to zero
Fully managed — points at the SFT job's pre-merged hf_merged/ weights
Blocked in the workshop account (SCP) → run in us-east-1/2, us-west-2, or eu-central-1
